lessons.title: Lesson 1: Noting Story Grammar
lessons.lectureText: Today we will learn about the parts of a story. Every story has characters, a setting, a problem, events, and a resolution. Let's read a short passage about Mang Berto and talk about its story elements.

parts.passageText: Mang Berto's Story Mang Berto is a factory worker. He works in a candy factory in Laguna. He wakes up early and walks every morning to go to work. How hard Mang Berto works in the factory! Then, he walks back to his house to eat dinner with his family. Why does he walk every day? Mang Berto wants to save money for his family. What a great man! Do you also save money like Mang Berto?

miniQuestions.questionText: Characters: Who is the main character in the story?
Setting: Where does Mang Berto work?
Problem: What challenge does Mang Berto face every day?

choices.choiceText: He wants to save money for his family

miniQuestions.questionText: Events: Arrange Mang Berto's day in the correct order.

choices.choiceText: He walks every morning to go to work.
He works hard in the candy factory.
He walks back home after work.
He eats dinner with his family.

miniQuestions.questionText: Resolution: How does Mang Berto solve his problem of saving money?

choices.choiceText: By walking to work every day instead of riding
He asks his boss for a raise

parts.passageText: Now that we've discussed the story elements in Mang Berto's story, let's read another passage and practice identifying the story grammar on our own. L1 ACTIVITY: Map Me Out! At the School Grounds Jo and his friends were playing baseball at the school grounds. They were having so much fun. When the ball was hit, it flew into the air, and Jo ran to catch it. Instead of catching the ball, it hit his head. He fell to the ground. "My head really hurts," he told his teacher. His teacher brought him to the school nurse. She gave him an ice pack for his head. Soon, his head began to feel better.

activityQuestions.questionText: 1. Characters: Who are the characters in the story?

choices.choiceText: Jo, his friends, his teacher, and the school nurse
Jo and the school nurse only

activityQuestions.questionText: 2. Setting: Where did the story happen?
3. Problem: What problem did Jo face while playing baseball?

choices.choiceText: The ball hit Jo's head and it hurt

activityQuestions.questionText: 4. Events: Arrange what happened first, next, and last in the story.

choices.choiceText: Jo and his friends played baseball.
The ball hit Jo's head, and he told his teacher.
The nurse gave him an ice pack, and he felt better.

activityQuestions.questionText: 5. Resolution: How was Jo's problem solved?

choices.choiceText: The school nurse gave him an ice pack, and his head began to feel better
His friends carried him to the clinic

lessons.title: Lesson 2: Author's Point of View
lessons.lectureText: Point of view means who is telling the story. First Person — The storyteller is inside the story. Uses words like I, me, we, my. Example: I walked to school. Second Person — The storyteller is talking to you. Uses words like you, your. Example: You wake up early. Third Person — The storyteller is outside the story. Uses words like he, she, they, their. Example: Jo played baseball with his friends. Third Person Omniscient (All-Knowing) — The storyteller knows everything about all characters. Can tell what everyone is thinking and feeling. Example: Jo was excited to play, but his teacher worried he might get hurt.

parts.passageText: Ana is a Grade 4 pupil at Masipag Elementary School. Her teachers always say that she is one of the most industrious pupils in their class. They also say that she is caring. Whenever someone needs help, she is always ready to lend a hand.

miniQuestions.questionText: What point of view is used in Ana's passage?
Which pronoun clue tells you it is Third Person POV?

parts.passageText: My favorite subjects are English, Mathematics, and Science. English is one of my favorite subjects because I love reading stories and writing stories about fairy tales. Next is Mathematics because when I was still young, my mother taught me about adding, subtracting, multiplying, and even dividing. My interest in science began when we had a field trip in a zoo where I have had a lot of 'why' questions about the animals. I always top the class in these subjects, and I really work hard to love the other subjects as well. --- You wake up early because today is the town fiesta. You hear the sound of drums outside your house. You quickly put on your best clothes and run to the plaza. At the plaza, you see colorful banderitas hanging above you.

miniQuestions.questionText: What point of view is used in the 'My favorite subjects' passage?
What point of view is used in the fiesta passage ('You wake up early...')?
Which word clue signals 2nd Person POV?

activityQuestions.questionText: 1. 'My biggest dream in life is to become a doctor. I love watching movies where doctors are happy about saving the lives of people.' — What is the POV?
2. 'You walk into your classroom early in the morning. You place your bag on the desk and greet your classmates with a smile.' — What is the POV?
3. 'Mrs. Reyes is the best teacher. She always makes sure our classroom looks good. Sometimes she rewards us with chocolates.' — What is the POV?
4. 'Joseph and his friends went to Manila Zoo. They had fun watching the different animals. Joseph even made a list of animals and took pictures.' — What is the POV?
5. 'Nida is a helpful child. She always helps her mother in cooking and cleaning the house. Sometimes she helps his father in the store during her free time.' — What is the POV?

lessons.title: Lesson 3: Sequencing Events
lessons.lectureText: When you wake up, what do you do first? Do you eat breakfast before brushing your teeth, or after? Sequencing means putting events in the correct order — what happens first, next, and last. It's like following steps in cooking or playing a game. Mini example: 1. You wash your hands. 2. You eat lunch. 3. You go to school. Stories also have a beginning, middle, and end. If we mix them up, the story is confusing. Sequencing helps us understand the story clearly.

parts.passageText: Grandfather's Old Watch by: Syrelle France S. Paterter 1. You and your sister play with cousins at your grandparents' house. 2. Kuya Anton pushes you and Alyana on the swings. 3. You see your grandfather looking at his old watch. 4. You ask him why he doesn't replace it, and he smiles. 5. When your grandfather dies, the watch is placed in his casket. 6. Your father explains the watch was a wedding gift from your Lola.

miniQuestions.questionText: Family Picnic — Arrange the events in the correct order:

choices.choiceText: You wake up early and help pack food.
Your family rides the jeepney to the park.
You spread the mat under a big mango tree.
You eat adobo, rice, and fruits together.
You play patintero with your cousins.
You take pictures with your parents before going home.

parts.passageText: Now that you know how to sequence events, let's head to the activity! L3 ACTIVITY: Arrange the passages to their correct order Morning Routine: • You eat pandesal with cheese. • You prepare your school bag. • You brush your teeth. • You put on your uniform. • You wake up early. • You wash your face.

activityQuestions.questionText: 1. Morning Routine — Arrange in the correct order:
2. Planting a Seed — Arrange in the correct order:

choices.choiceText: You dig a small hole in the garden.
You place the seed inside the hole.
You cover the seed with soil.
You water the soil every day.
You see the seed grow into a plant.
You watch the plant grow taller.

activityQuestions.questionText: 3. Riding a Jeepney — Arrange in the correct order:

choices.choiceText: You hand your fare to the driver.
You get off at your destination.

activityQuestions.questionText: 4. The Zoo — Arrange in the correct order:

choices.choiceText: Joseph and his friends went to Manila Zoo.
They had fun watching the different animals.
They learned many things about the animals.
Joseph made a list of animals and took pictures.
Joseph was amazed to see many people visiting the zoo.
Joseph told his family about their trip.

activityQuestions.questionText: 5. Cinderella — Arrange in the correct order:

choices.choiceText: Cinderella was humiliated by her stepmother and stepsisters.
Prince Charming invited every woman to join the ball.
Cinderella's fairy godmother helped her attend the ball.
Cinderella danced all night with Prince Charming.
Cinderella hurriedly went home and left one glass shoe.
Prince Charming let every woman try on the shoe.

lessons.title: Lesson 4: Sequential Plot
lessons.lectureText: Class, have you ever told a story about your weekend? Did you notice that you usually start with what happened first, then what happened next, and finally what happened last? That's called sequencing. Today, we will learn how to identify the type of plot called sequential. A sequential plot is a story where events happen in order — beginning, middle, and end. We look at what happens first, next, and last. This helps us understand the story clearly.

parts.passageText: Our Family Bonding By: Syrelle France S. Paterter Every weekend, our family usually goes near the river for a picnic. My father always brings with him his fishing rod so we can catch and eat fresh fish. Kuya Nestor and I often help our mother in preparing the picnic mats and all the food. Sometimes, we sing our favorite songs while Kuya plays the guitar. This is how we bond and catch up after a whole week at school and work.

miniQuestions.questionText: What happened first in the story?

choices.choiceText: The family went near the river for a picnic.

miniQuestions.questionText: What did Father bring with him?
What did Kuya Nestor and the narrator do?

choices.choiceText: They helped their mother prepare picnic mats and food.

miniQuestions.questionText: What did the family do while Kuya played the guitar?
What was the last thing mentioned in the story?

choices.choiceText: The family bonded and caught up after a week at school and work.
They packed up and went home.

parts.passageText: Today we learned that a sequential plot shows events in order. The picnic story is sequential because it starts with going to the river, then catching fish, preparing food, singing songs, and ends with family bonding. Remember: sequencing helps us understand stories better. L4 ACTIVITY: The Real Champion By: Syrelle France S. Paterter Aya Shanelle is a graceful dancer. She loves to join events such as dancing competitions. There are times that she ends up being the loser of the event. But that didn't stop her from becoming more passionate about her craft. She continuously joins more exciting dance competitions. Win or lose, it didn't matter. What matters for her the most are the lessons learned and friends gained in every contest. That is the true essence of being a real champion.

activityQuestions.questionText: 1. What is Aya Shanelle's talent?
2. What happened first in the story?

choices.choiceText: Aya Shanelle is introduced as a graceful dancer who loves joining competitions.
She values lessons learned and friends.
She becomes passionate about her craft.

activityQuestions.questionText: 3. What does Aya Shanelle gain from joining contests, aside from winning?
4. Sequencing: Arrange these 6 events in the correct order.

choices.choiceText: Aya Shanelle is a graceful dancer.
She loves to join dancing competitions.
She becomes more passionate about her craft.
She continuously joins more exciting competitions.
She values lessons learned and friends gained.

activityQuestions.questionText: 5. At the end of the story, what does Aya Shanelle realize is the true essence of being a champion?

choices.choiceText: That lessons learned and friends gained in every contest matter most.
That winning every competition is the only goal.
That being popular is more important than growing.
That practicing hard guarantees a win.

lessons.title: Lesson 5: Drawing Conclusions
lessons.lectureText: Class, sometimes a story doesn't tell us everything directly. We need to look at the clues in the text and use our own thinking to figure out what the author wants us to understand. This skill is called drawing conclusions. It's like solving a puzzle — we put the pieces together to see the bigger picture. Drawing conclusions means combining what the story says with what we already know. We don't just read the words; we think about what they mean. Example: If you see someone watering plants every day, what can you conclude? They care about their garden.

parts.passageText: Home by: Ma. Francia N. Bulacan My family and I live in a far but peaceful town. We live in a small hut surrounded by plants. We help each other in keeping our home neat and clean. Father and I work on our garden every morning. It is filled with green, leafy vegetables. We built a sturdy fence around it and painted it blue. Mother has her garden too. Our front lawn is filled with fragrant and colorful flowers. Mother and sister have pink, clay pots. They remove the weeds that grow, water them every day, and put fertilizer too. We feel happy looking at all these things growing around the house. It makes the surroundings look happy and lively. It feels fun helping one another in keeping our home beautiful and filling it with love too.

miniQuestions.questionText: The family built a sturdy fence and painted it blue. What can we conclude about how they feel toward their garden?

choices.choiceText: They care for it and want it protected and beautiful.
They want to sell the garden.
They just like the color blue.

miniQuestions.questionText: Father and the narrator work in the vegetable garden every morning. What can we conclude about their daily routine?

choices.choiceText: They are hardworking and value fresh food from their garden.
They have no jobs to go to.
They are forced to work in the garden.

miniQuestions.questionText: Mother and sister water the flowers every day and add fertilizer. What can we conclude about their attitude toward plants?

choices.choiceText: They are responsible and love making their surroundings colorful.
They think plants are a waste of time.
They water plants only when they remember.

miniQuestions.questionText: The family feels happy looking at the plants around their house. What can we conclude about the role of nature in their lives?

choices.choiceText: Nature brings them joy, peace, and togetherness.
They only keep plants for selling.
They feel nature is too much work.

miniQuestions.questionText: The story shows everyone helping one another in keeping the home beautiful. What can we conclude about the family's values?

choices.choiceText: They value cooperation, love, and working together as a family.

parts.passageText: We learned that drawing conclusions means looking at the clues in the story and thinking about what they mean. In this story, the family's actions show us they value love, cooperation, and caring for nature. That's the conclusion we can draw. L5 ACTIVITY: Buddy and the Cat Little Buddy Cockroach is a house bug. Mother Cockroach always tells him to stay in dark places and hide. He listens to her with half an ear not believing in what she says. "How could they not like me? I have beautiful, shiny wings, and long antenna. Surely, they would love me," he thought. One day, Buddy saw a black cat and said, "I will show Mother Cockroach that she is wrong." But when the cat saw him, it immediately jumped and started running after him with its sharp claws. Buddy ran as fast as he could to the dark place under the cabinet and never came out.

activityQuestions.questionText: 1. Buddy thought people would love him because of his shiny wings and long antenna. What can we conclude about Buddy's personality?

choices.choiceText: He is proud and overconfident about his appearance.

activityQuestions.questionText: 2. Mother Cockroach always told Buddy to stay in dark places and hide. What can we conclude about why she gave this advice?

choices.choiceText: She knows cockroaches are disliked and unsafe in the open, so she wanted to protect him.
She wanted Buddy to be alone.
She was jealous of Buddy's wings.
She didn't want Buddy to have friends.

activityQuestions.questionText: 3. Buddy ignored his mother's warning and approached the cat. What can we conclude about Buddy's attitude toward his mother's advice?

choices.choiceText: He was careless and disobedient — he didn't believe her.
He agreed with his mother but forgot her advice.

activityQuestions.questionText: 4. The cat immediately chased Buddy with sharp claws. What can we conclude about how animals or people usually react to cockroaches?

choices.choiceText: They see cockroaches as pests or threats and try to get rid of them.

activityQuestions.questionText: 5. Buddy ran to the dark place under the cabinet and never came out. What can we conclude about what Buddy learned from this experience?

choices.choiceText: He realized his mother was right, and it is safer to stay hidden.
He decided to find a new home.
He planned to challenge the cat again.
He forgot what happened to him.

lessons.title: Lesson 6: Identifying the Main Idea
lessons.lectureText: Class, when we read, we don't just look at the details. We need to find the main idea — the big thought that the author wants us to understand. The details are like ingredients in a recipe, but the main idea is the finished dish. Today, we'll learn how to identify the main idea in informational texts. The main idea is the most important point of the text. Supporting details give us examples, descriptions, or lists that explain the main idea.

parts.passageText: Filipino food is known for its variety and flavor. One popular dish is Adobo, cooked with soy sauce, vinegar, garlic, and meat. Another favorite is Sinigang, a sour soup made with tamarind, vegetables, and either pork or shrimp. Filipinos also enjoy Lechon, a roasted pig served during fiestas. For snacks, there is Bibingka, a rice cake baked in banana leaves, and Halo-Halo, a cold dessert with ice, milk, fruits, and sweet beans. These dishes show how Filipino food is colorful, flavorful, and enjoyed by families everywhere.

miniQuestions.questionText: What ingredients are used in Adobo?

choices.choiceText: Soy sauce, vinegar, garlic, and meat

miniQuestions.questionText: What makes Sinigang sour?
What dish is served during fiestas?
What are two examples of Filipino snacks in the passage?
Based on all the details, what is the main idea of the Filipino food passage?

choices.choiceText: Filipino food is flavorful and varied, with many popular dishes enjoyed by families.
Adobo is the most popular Filipino food.
Filipinos love eating snacks every day.
Sinigang is the best Filipino dish.

parts.passageText: Now that we've learned how to find the main idea in an informational text, we will discuss what is informational text. What is Informational Text? Informational text is non-fiction writing. It gives facts, explanations, or descriptions about real things, people, places, or events. It is not a made-up story; instead, it teaches or informs the reader. Examples of Informational Texts: How Plants Grow Plants need sunlight, water, and soil to grow. First, the roots take in water and nutrients from the soil. Next, the stem carries these nutrients to the leaves. The leaves use sunlight to make food through photosynthesis. Finally, flowers and fruits appear, showing that the plant is healthy and strong. Main Idea: Plants grow by using water, soil, and sunlight to make food. Philippine Fruits The Philippines has many delicious fruits. One is Mango, which is sweet and yellow when ripe. Another is Banana, soft and filling, often eaten for breakfast. There is also Papaya, orange in color and rich in vitamins. Filipinos enjoy Coconut, which has refreshing water and white meat. Finally, Lanzones are small, round fruits that taste sweet and slightly sour. Main Idea: The Philippines has many kinds of tasty fruits. Types of Informational Text: 1. Enumeration: means listing details one by one. It uses words like first, second, next, also, another. It helps readers see the information clearly in order. 2. Description: means explaining how something looks, tastes, smells, or feels. It uses adjectives (describing words) to give details.

Now that you've discussed how to find the main idea and the types of informational texts, let's head to the activity and try it on our own. Have Fun! L6 ACTIVITY Read each short passage. Then decide: Is it Enumeration (listing things one by one) or Description (explaining how something looks, tastes, smells, or feels)? The Filipino people are very fond of festivals and celebrations. The Panagbenga Festival in Baguio City is celebrated in February. People from far and near visit the place to see the parade of floats that are decorated with fragrant and multi-colored flowers. The Pampanga's Hot Air Balloon Festival is also a fun and colorful activity. It happens yearly between January and February at the Clark Freeport Zone.

activityQuestions.questionText: Read the passage: 'The Philippines has many fruits. First, there is the mango. Second, there is the banana. Third, there is the papaya. Finally, there is the coconut.' — Enumeration or Description?
'The mango is sweet, yellow, and juicy when ripe. It has a smooth skin and a pleasant smell.' — Enumeration or Description?
'Filipino houses often have gardens. Some grow vegetables, others plant flowers, and many keep fruit trees.' — Enumeration or Description?
'The Sinigang soup tastes sour because of tamarind. It is hot, comforting, and filled with vegetables and meat. The smell is strong but delicious.' — Enumeration or Description?
'During Christmas, Filipinos prepare different foods. They cook ham, serve rice cakes, make spaghetti, and prepare fruit salad.' — Enumeration or Description?
6. When is the Panagbenga Festival celebrated?
7. What do people see during the Panagbenga Festival parade?

choices.choiceText: Floats decorated with fragrant and multi-colored flowers

activityQuestions.questionText: 8. Where does the Pampanga's Hot Air Balloon Festival take place?
9. When does the Pampanga's Hot Air Balloon Festival usually happen?
10. Based on the festival passage, what is the main idea?

choices.choiceText: Filipino people love colorful festivals and celebrations.
Hot air balloons are hard to fly.
Filipinos love food more than festivals.

lessons.title: Lesson 7: Making a Summary
lessons.lectureText: Class, when we read, we see many details. But sometimes we only need the most important points. This skill is called making a summary. A summary is a short version of the text that tells the big idea and the most important details. It leaves out extra words and examples. Example: 'If I tell you: Maria bought balloons, cake, and gifts for her party, how can we summarize it? We can say: Maria prepared for her party. That's the summary.' Summary = short version of the text. It includes the main idea and the most important details. It does not include every single word or minor detail.

parts.passageText: Birthdays are special celebrations in many places. In the Philippines, families prepare food like pancit, spaghetti, and fried chicken. Children often receive gifts such as toys and clothes. Homes are decorated with balloons and colorful banners. A birthday cake with candles is placed at the center of the table. Friends and relatives gather to sing, eat, and enjoy games together. These traditions make birthdays joyful and memorable.

miniQuestions.questionText: What foods are usually prepared during birthdays in the Philippines?
What do children often receive as gifts?
What decorations are used in homes during birthdays?
What is placed at the center of the table?
If you put all the important details together, what is the summary of the passage?

choices.choiceText: Birthdays in the Philippines are joyful celebrations with food, gifts, decorations, cake, and family gatherings.
Filipinos only eat pancit on birthdays.
Birthdays are celebrated with fireworks in the Philippines.
Children receive money as birthday gifts.

parts.passageText: We learned that making a summary means telling the big idea and the most important details in a shorter way. In our passage, the details about food, gifts, decorations, cake, and family gatherings all point to one summary: Birthdays are joyful celebrations filled with traditions. L7 ACTIVITY: Map Me Out! One sunny morning at Mabuti Elementary School, Zarie was the only pupil who comes to school the earliest. She saw the canteen manager, Aling Nena, cleaning the canteen. She helped Aling Nena clean the canteen. As Zarie is sweeping the floor, she found a one hundred peso bill and gave it to Aling Nena who thanked her for the honest deed she did. As a reward, Aling Nena gave Zarie a pouch of chocolates. She thanked Aling Nena for the reward she received. --- It was Carlo's 10th birthday. His family decorated their home with balloons and colorful streamers. On the table, there was pancit for long life, fried chicken, and a big chocolate cake with ten candles. Carlo's cousins brought gifts wrapped in bright paper. After singing 'Happy Birthday,' Carlo blew out the candles and made a wish. Everyone clapped, ate together, and played games. The celebration was filled with laughter and joy.

activityQuestions.questionText: Who was cleaning the canteen when Zarie arrived?
What did Zarie find while sweeping the floor?
What can we conclude about Zarie's character based on her actions?
Why do you think Aling Nena gave Zarie chocolates as a reward?

choices.choiceText: To show appreciation for Zarie's honesty and kindness

activityQuestions.questionText: What is the best summary of the Zarie passage?

choices.choiceText: Zarie helped clean the canteen, found money, returned it honestly to Aling Nena, and was rewarded with chocolates.
Zarie went to school early and saw Aling Nena.
Zarie found money and spent it on chocolates.
Aling Nena gave chocolates to all early students.

activityQuestions.questionText: What foods were served at Carlo's birthday?

choices.choiceText: Pancit, fried chicken, and chocolate cake

activityQuestions.questionText: How many candles were on Carlo's cake?
Why do Filipinos serve pancit during birthdays?

choices.choiceText: Because it is cheap to cook
Because it is easy to prepare

activityQuestions.questionText: What can we conclude about Carlo's family based on the way they celebrated?

choices.choiceText: They value togetherness, joy, and tradition.
They prefer simple celebrations without guests.

activityQuestions.questionText: What is the best summary of Carlo's birthday story?

choices.choiceText: Carlo's family celebrated his 10th birthday with food, decorations, gifts, cake, and games, showing love and joy.
The family ate pancit and played games.

lessons.title: Lesson 8: Fact and Opinion
lessons.lectureText: Narrative texts are stories. These are the stories that your mother and father read or tell you. This lesson will teach you how to distinguish fact from opinion in a narrative. What is a fact? A fact is something that is true and supported by evidence. It speaks about something that is true and based on evidence. What is an opinion? An opinion is only a belief. It is also an expression of a person's feelings or point of view. Clue words like 'think,' 'believe,' 'feel,' 'probably,' or 'seems' signal an opinion. Example: Perhaps only sons should be treated well.

parts.passageText: Simple Celebration Lea E. Basquinas "Hello, Kumareng Connie and Kumpareng Kardo. Good morning!" said Aling Myrna. "How are your children?" "Oh, hi there Mareng Myrna. Good morning too," replied Aling Connie. "Our children are okay. Ric is now in the army. Ana is now studying education and our youngest Ada is in grade 4," said Mang Kardo. "I came here to invite your family personally, Mareng Connie," said Aling Myrna. "There will be a thanksgiving party in our house tomorrow evening, just a simple celebration because I am promoted as supervisor of the LEBB Corporation." "Congratulations Mareng Myrna! I believe you are really fit in that position," greeted Aling Connie. "Thank you, Mareng Connie and Pareng Kardo. I think my children will be glad to see you with your children," replied Aling Myrna.

miniQuestions.questionText: Who are the characters in the story?

choices.choiceText: Mang Kardo, Aling Connie, and Aling Myrna
Mang Kardo, Aling Connie, and Riza
Mang Kardo and Aling Myrna only

miniQuestions.questionText: What is the celebration in Aling Myrna's house?
Why did Aling Myrna invite Aling Connie's family?
Which sentence expresses a FACT?

choices.choiceText: There will be a thanksgiving party at our house tomorrow.
I believe you are really fit in that position.
It seems the children would love to attend.
I think my children will be glad to see you.

miniQuestions.questionText: Which sentence expresses an OPINION?

choices.choiceText: I think my children will be glad to see you.
There will be a thanksgiving party at our house tomorrow.
I am promoted as supervisor of the LEBB Corporation.
Riza my daughter and Anna your daughter are classmates.

parts.passageText: Sentences like 'There will be a thanksgiving party' or 'Aling Myrna was promoted' express FACT because they actually happened in the story. Sentences with words like believe or think express OPINION because they signal a point of view. L8 ACTIVITY: Boys and Girls By: Lea E. Basquinas At school, during English class, Mr. Mahinay asked the class to group themselves and make a thank you card as an output. All the groups were busy doing their output. "Jilian, do you have another bond paper? May I ask for 2 pieces please," asked Zaira. "I think I have plenty. Here, take 3 pieces," said Jilian. "I believe your card is more beautiful than mine," said Zaira. "Perhaps Jilian's thank you card is the most beautiful," replied Zarie. "I don't think so," said Jilian. "I think all of our thank-you cards are beautiful." "Jilian is right," declared Mr. Mahinay. "Every artwork is unique. Just like you little girls, you are all beautiful." L8 REVIEW Fill in the blanks with the correct answers: fact, true, opinion, believe, seems. A fact is something that is true and supported by evidence. An opinion is only a belief. It may contain clue words like 'think,' 'believe,' 'feel,' 'probably,' or 'seems.'

activityQuestions.questionText: 1. 'It seems that Jilian's thank you card is the most beautiful.' — FACT or OPINION?
2. 'I think I have plenty.' — FACT or OPINION?
3. 'I believe your card is more beautiful than mine.' — FACT or OPINION?
4. 'Mr. Mahinay asked the class to group themselves and make a thank you card as an output.' — FACT or OPINION?
5. 'I think all of our thank you cards are beautiful.' — FACT or OPINION?
A ____________ is something that is true and supported by evidence.
An ____________ is only a belief and expression of feelings. Clue words: think, believe, seems.

lessons.title: Lesson 9: Author's Purpose
lessons.lectureText: In this lesson, you will learn to identify the text's purpose. An author usually writes to achieve one of the following main goals: 1. Entertain — To make the reader enjoy reading through stories, amusing events, or vibrant characters. 2. Inform — To give the reader true facts and information about a specific topic. 3. Describe — To paint a picture in the reader's mind using sensory details (sight, sound, smell, touch, and taste).

parts.passageText: Examples: 1. ENTERTAIN: One sunny afternoon, Maya found a tiny talking turtle beside the river. 'Can you help me find my way home?' the turtle asked. Maya laughed in surprise and followed the turtle into the forest. Along the way, they met dancing monkeys and colorful birds. 2. INFORM: The Philippine Eagle is one of the largest eagles in the world. It can only be found in the Philippines. This eagle lives in forests and eats small animals like monkeys and snakes. Sadly, the Philippine Eagle is endangered because many forests are being cut down. 3. DESCRIBE: The tall glass of halo-halo was a beautiful masterpiece of bright purple ube, yellow sweet corn, and deep red beans. Crushed ice glistened on top like tiny diamonds, drenched in creamy, sweet milk that felt delightfully cold and refreshing.

miniQuestions.questionText: 'The mango tree gives sweet fruits during summer. Many Filipino families enjoy eating ripe mangoes as snacks. Mangoes are rich in vitamins.' — Text Purpose?
'The sand on the beach was pure white and felt warm between my toes. The clear turquoise water sparkled brightly under the golden sun.' — Text Purpose?
'Ben tried to fly his homemade kite. Suddenly, a mischievous squirrel grabbed the kite string and tried to fly away with it!' — Text Purpose?
'A solar eclipse happens when the moon moves directly between the Earth and the sun. The moon blocks the sunlight, making the sky darker.' — Text Purpose?
'The traditional bahay kubo is built with textured brown bamboo. Inside, the smooth floor feels cool, and wide windows let in the fresh, earthy scent of the garden.' — Text Purpose?
'When Carlo started writing with his magic pencil, his drawings jumped off the paper! A little paper dog began barking and chasing his eraser.' — Text Purpose?

parts.passageText: Now that we've discussed the three main purposes of an author (Entertain, Inform, Describe), let's head to the activity and identify the purpose of each passage on our own. L9 ACTIVITY

activityQuestions.questionText: 1. 'The carabao is a helpful animal commonly found in farms in the Philippines. It is strong, hardworking, and used by farmers to plow rice fields.' — Purpose?
2. 'Last Sunday, Ben heard a strange sound outside. When he opened the door, he saw a monkey holding bananas from their kitchen basket.' — Purpose?
3. 'Mayon Volcano is located in Albay. It is famous for its perfect cone shape and is one of the most active volcanoes in the Philippines.' — Purpose?
4. 'The beach in Batangas had soft white sand and clear blue water. Tall coconut trees swayed gently while the cool wind blew across the shore.' — Purpose?
5. 'The Philippine eagle is one of the largest eagles in the world. It can be found in forests in Mindanao and is considered an endangered animal.' — Purpose?
6. 'During the barangay fiesta, Carlo accidentally dropped the lechon sauce on his shirt. Everyone laughed when their dog started licking the sauce from the floor.' — Purpose?
7. 'The jeepney is a popular mode of transportation in the Philippines. It can carry many passengers and is known for its colorful decorations.' — Purpose?
8. 'The chocolate cake was soft, sweet, and covered with thick creamy icing. Colorful sprinkles were placed on top of the cake.' — Purpose?
9. 'One evening, Liza heard loud noises from the roof during a brownout. She discovered it was only a cat running above the house.' — Purpose?
10. 'Rice terraces can be found in the mountains of Ifugao. They were built by ancestors many years ago and are important tourist attractions today.' — Purpose?

lessons.title: Lesson 10: Making Predictions
lessons.lectureText: Making a prediction means using clues from the text to guess what will happen next. Example: Sara's dad opens the fridge and grabs bread, slices of turkey, cheese, and mustard. He also grabs a napkin and a small plastic bag. What probably happened next? The answer is: Sara's dad made her a sandwich — because those are the clues in the paragraph. Remember: We use clues from the story to make our prediction, not just a guess!

parts.passageText: Example 1: Everyone was having fun at the magic show. Then the magician said, 'I need a volunteer for this next amazing act of magic.' Lots of kids jumped up and raised their hands. The magician pointed to Cindy. 'You, there,' he said. What probably happened next? a. Cindy went up on the stage. b. Cindy sat back down. c. The magician took a bow. d. The magician pulled a rabbit out of a hat. Answer: a. Cindy went up on the stage. Example 2: The school play ended. Everyone had remembered their lines. The audience had laughed at all the right places. The lights came on and the students all came back on stage and took a bow. Kim woke up on Saturday morning. She remembered she had a few chores to do before she went out to play, but she didn't feel so good. She still felt tired, and her stomach hurt. 'I don't feel so good, Mom,' she said. 'Oh,' said Mom. 'I don't think you'll be doing chores or going out to play today.'

miniQuestions.questionText: School Play: What probably happened next after the students took a bow?
Kim's Story: What probably happened next after Mom's statement?

choices.choiceText: Kim helped her mom wash the dishes.

parts.passageText: Great job! Now that you've finished the warm-up, you are ready for the Activity right? Let's go! L10 ACTIVITY

activityQuestions.questionText: 1. It was almost 4:00 in the afternoon when Bea heard the familiar bell of the taho vendor outside their house. She quickly grabbed some coins from the table and ran to the gate. What will probably happen next?

2. Jun and his classmates practiced every day for the Buwan ng Wika dance presentation. They memorized all the steps and wore colorful Filipino costumes during the program. What will probably happen next?

choices.choiceText: The class will perform their dance on stage.
The students will take a math test.

activityQuestions.questionText: 3. The rain became stronger while Carlo was on his way home from school. He opened his bag and looked for the umbrella his mother told him to bring. What will probably happen next?
4. During lunch break, Maya opened her baon and smelled her favorite chicken adobo. Her friends looked at the food and smiled. What will probably happen next?

choices.choiceText: Maya will eat her lunch happily.
Maya will throw away her baon.
Maya will start cleaning the classroom.
Maya will go to the library.

activityQuestions.questionText: 5. The barangay announced that there would be no classes tomorrow because of the approaching typhoon. Nico immediately checked the batteries of the flashlight and helped his mother store water. What will probably happen next?

choices.choiceText: Nico's family will prepare for the typhoon.
Nico will go to the beach.
Nico will fly a kite outside.
Nico will play under the heavy rain.

pre_post_questions.questionText: Which part of the story tells us where and when it takes place?
In the sentence, "I felt very nervous before the big game," what is the point of view?
Which story element describes the main struggle or challenge?
When a narrator uses the word "You" and talks to the reader, they are using:
The Resolution of a story is the part where:
What is the purpose of Sequencing?

choices.choiceText: To count the words in a story
To put events in the correct order

pre_post_questions.questionText: Which signal word is used to show the start of a process?
Which word signals that the final step is happening?
A Sequential Plot is a story that moves:

choices.choiceText: From the end back to the beginning
From the start to the end in time order

pre_post_questions.questionText: If you are following a recipe to bake a cake, you are reading a:
"The moon was a silver coin in the sky." This is a:
A metaphor is a comparison that:

choices.choiceText: Says one thing is another thing

pre_post_questions.questionText: Which word is an example of Onomatopoeia (sound word)?
The phrase "Peter Piper picked a peck" uses which sound device?
Why do authors use Rhyme in poetry?

choices.choiceText: To make it harder to read
To create a musical pattern and rhythm

pre_post_questions.questionText: If a character is wearing a swimsuit and carrying a towel, you can conclude:

choices.choiceText: They are going to the beach or pool.

pre_post_questions.questionText: To draw a conclusion, you use clues from the text plus your:
A prediction is a guess about:

choices.choiceText: What will happen next in the story

pre_post_questions.questionText: If a dark cloud covers the sun and the wind picks up, what do you predict?

choices.choiceText: It will be a hot day.

pre_post_questions.questionText: Conclusions are often:

choices.choiceText: Written directly in the first sentence
Not stated directly; you have to figure them out

pre_post_questions.questionText: Which of the following is a Fact?

choices.choiceText: "Yellow is a better color than red."

pre_post_questions.questionText: Which word usually signals an Opinion?
An Informational Recount is used to:
Which text type uses many adjectives to tell how something looks or feels?
In an Enumeration text, the author:

choices.choiceText: Tells a story about a hero
Lists items or parts of a whole

pre_post_questions.questionText: A Summary should be:

choices.choiceText: A short version with only the main points
A list of all the adjectives used
Just the last sentence of the story

pre_post_questions.questionText: When an author writes to Inform, they provide:
If an author writes a story to make you laugh, their purpose is to:
To summarize, we often look for the 5 W's: Who, What, Where, When, and:
Identifying the Author's Purpose helps us:

choices.choiceText: Understand why the text was written

pre_post_questions.questionText: Which element refers to the people, animals, or creatures that take part in the action of a story?
The "Resolution" of a story is the part where:

choices.choiceText: The story reaches its scariest part
The main problem is finally solved
The story moves to a new location

pre_post_questions.questionText: Which point of view uses pronouns like "I," "me," "we," and "us"?
If a narrator talks directly to you using the word "You," which point of view is being used?
"The king was sad, but the queen felt hopeful." If the narrator knows the feelings of all characters, it is:
What do we call the logical order of events in a story?
Which signal word is most likely to be found in the Middle of a sequence?
In a Sequential Plot, how do events move?

choices.choiceText: They follow a clear timeline from start to finish
They only show the ending first
They repeat the same event over and over

pre_post_questions.questionText: Which of these is the first step in a sequence?

choices.choiceText: Finally, we arrived at the beach.
To begin with, we packed our bags.
After that, we drove for two hours.

pre_post_questions.questionText: Why are signal words important in a procedural text?

choices.choiceText: They help the reader follow steps in the right order.
They describe what the characters look like.
They tell us the author's name.

pre_post_questions.questionText: "The stars were small diamonds in the dark sky." This is a:
A metaphor is a comparison that DOES NOT use which words?
Which sound device is used in the phrase: "The pitter-patter of pink petals"?
Which of these is an example of Onomatopoeia?
What is the effect of Repetition in a poem?

choices.choiceText: It emphasizes a certain feeling or idea.
It provides a list of facts.
It changes the point of view.

pre_post_questions.questionText: If you see a student crying while looking at a broken toy, what can you conclude?

choices.choiceText: They are upset because the toy is special to them.
They are going to buy a new car.

pre_post_questions.questionText: Drawing a conclusion is often called:
A prediction is a guess about:

choices.choiceText: Why the author wrote the book
What will happen next based on clues

pre_post_questions.questionText: A character puts on a helmet and gets on a bicycle. What do you predict?

choices.choiceText: They will go for a swim.
They will go for a bike ride.

pre_post_questions.questionText: To make a good prediction, you need:

choices.choiceText: Clues from the story + your own experiences (Schema)
The price of the book + the author’s name

pre_post_questions.questionText: Which sentence is an Opinion?

choices.choiceText: Ice cream is the best dessert in the world.

pre_post_questions.questionText: Which word is a clue that a sentence is an Opinion?
A Description text type uses many:
What is the purpose of an Informational Recount?

choices.choiceText: To tell a story about talking animals
To retell a real event that happened in the past
To persuade you to buy something
To teach you how to draw

pre_post_questions.questionText: An Enumeration text type:

choices.choiceText: Tells a story with a problem

pre_post_questions.questionText: Which of the following should NOT be in a summary?
The "5 W's" used in summarizing are Who, What, Where, When, and:
If an author writes a brochure about "How to stay safe during a typhoon," their purpose is to:
If an author writes a story about a dragon and a knight, their purpose is to:
Understanding the Author’s Purpose helps the reader:

choices.choiceText: Know if the information is a fact or just for fun
